home.hero_subtitle: FarmDrop is a collaborative online marketplace connecting Maine farmers directly to communities. Farmers get paid up front. Customers shop once a week. Pickup happens at a regional hub. Zero waste, year-round.
home.how_intro: A farmers' market and a CSA had an online baby. That is FarmDrop.
home.step1_body: Local producers list everything they grow or make - no shelf-space limits. Unit sizes from a pint of jam to a whole lamb.
home.step2_body: Shop online through your regional FarmDrop. Farmers get paid the moment you check out, not after delivery.
home.step3_body: Farmers deliver in bulk to a regional Market Hub Manager who packs individual orders. You pick up midweek at your location.
home.step4_body: Farmers only bring what is sold. Customers only get what is fresh-harvested that morning. Nothing spoils on shelves.
home.teaser_body: Two FarmDrop fundraisers for Food for Health (May 24 Soulbenders benefit, Aug 6 Sassafras invite-only), plus a couple of community events across Downeast Maine where you can find Hannah.

hero.title: Gather. Eat. Build Maine's Food Future.
hero.subtitle: Two FarmDrop fundraisers for Food for Health, plus a couple of community gatherings around Downeast Maine where you can come say hi and talk local food systems with Hannah.

intro_cards.card3_label: Of every FarmDrop order to Food for Health

sections.farmdrop_intro: Two fundraisers to support Food for Health and the work of expanding Maine's farmer-to-table network.
sections.community_intro: FarmDrop is joining a few local gatherings this spring. Hannah Semler will be there to talk food systems, mutual aid, and how to support local farms. These are hosted by other organizations - come say hi.

may24.title: Soulbenders Benefit for Food for Health
may24.tags: Featured, Benefit, Live Music, Dance Party
may24.description: The Soulbenders play a fundraiser for FarmDrop's Food for Health program. Afternoon conversation on community food systems and mutual aid, partner-org tabling, and a dance party to close. Every dollar goes to Food for Health - buying surplus from Maine farms and routing it to families hit by SNAP cuts. Come hungry, come dancing.

aug6.title: Sam Lardner & Barcelona at Sassafras Cafe
aug6.description: A top-supporter evening with Sam Lardner & Barcelona. FarmDrop is raising funds for the infrastructure it takes to reach more Maine communities - vehicles, drivers, and cold storage to expand service into Washington County and beyond.

may9.title: Community Solutions Conversation
may9.description: A community conversation on mutual aid and food systems in Downeast Maine, hosted by Reversing Falls Sanctuary. Hannah Semler will speak on behalf of FarmDrop alongside other local organizations.

may23.title: Grocery List Documentary Screening
may23.description: A documentary screening hosted by Ellsworth Public Library as part of the Indie Lens Pop-Up series. FarmDrop will be on hand for the conversation afterward about where our food comes from and how local networks can replace long supply chains.

ffh.title: Food for Health - Three Ways to Give
ffh.intro: Formally launched in 2025 in response to SNAP reductions, Food for Health buys surplus from Maine farms and routes it through FarmDrop's existing delivery network to food security partner organizations across Downeast Maine. Three giving channels:
ffh.ch1_body: Every FarmDrop order you place automatically routes 2% to Food for Health. You don't have to do anything. You just have to shop.
ffh.ch2_body: Add any amount at checkout - it goes straight to Food for Health. Fastest way to feed a neighbor this week.
ffh.ch3_body: Select items priced so that buying one for yourself also funds one for a food-insecure neighbor through FFH's partner orgs.

interview.title: Listen: Hannah Semler on BCZ YapZ
interview.blurb: Thirty-minute conversation with FarmDrop CEO Hannah Semler on the origins of FarmDrop, the Food for Health program, and the spring and summer event lineup. Recorded April 22, 2026.

footer.tagline_events: Maine's mission-driven online farmers market. 65+ farm partners, 1,000+ customers, regional hubs across Maine.
footer.tagline_home: Maine's mission-driven online farmers market. Supporting local farmers and feeding communities across Maine.
footer.credit_line: FarmDrop | Led by CEO Hannah Semler | Event page by BetterCallZaal
